name: LaTeX
description: The standard document preparation system for physics. Every physics paper, thesis, and textbook is typeset in LaTeX. It handles mathematical notation beautifully.
whyUseIt: LaTeX produces publication-quality documents with perfect mathematical typesetting. It handles equation numbering, cross-references, citations, and figures automatically. Every physics journal requires submissions in LaTeX format.
fieldPosition: Universal — required for all physics publications, theses, and technical reports
physicistWorkflow: Write documents in a text editor with LaTeX markup, compile to PDF, manage references with BibTeX.
supervisorExpects: All written work submitted in LaTeX with proper equation formatting, references, and figure placement.
computationalImpact: Frees you from manual formatting — focus on content while LaTeX handles layout, numbering, and typography.
demoDescription: Write a short physics paper with numbered equations, cross-references, a bibliography, and properly formatted figures — all compiled from plain text.

vocabulary.definition: The section before \begin{document} where you load packages and set options
The reference management system used with LaTeX

beginnerMistakes: Fighting LaTeX's layout decisions instead of trusting the defaults
Not using version control (git) for LaTeX documents
